source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
## T2: Mechanical accelerator

A massless thread makes $N$ turns around statically fixed cylinder, as shown in the figure. Initially, the free (unwound) ends of the thread are parallel to the axis $X$. Then, a heavy point-like object $P$ is attached to one end of the thread while the other end is pulled with a constant velocity $u$ along $X$. Find the maximum velocity attained by the heavy object.

<image_1>

The thread is inextendable and flexible. Suppose that the turns of the thread are wound tightly to one another and are placed practically in the same plane, perpendicular to the cylinder axis. Neglect any friction in the system. Do not consider the force of gravity.
extraction_notes: image_1 exists as a PIL.Image placeholder rather than a path/URL, so no image path was extracted

the setup depends on spatial/geometric configuration of a thread wound around a cylinder and the axis X
multi_step: the problem asks for the maximum velocity of the object, which suggests deriving motion relations and then optimizing
the setup combines several constraints: N turns around a cylinder, inextendable thread, constant pull speed u, massless thread, no friction, no gravity
this is a mechanics/geometry word problem rather than direct lookup
answer is a symbolic closed-form expression in N and u, indicating nontrivial derivation
verifiability: answer_type is short_text and the provided answer is a specific formula
multiple-choice options are present, so the final result can be checked against listed candidates
the target output is a single symbolic expression, which is amenable to formula/equivalence checking
